title: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium
summary: CME00066 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected matter in Eugen Kölbing's 1877 Englische Studien article. The encoding starts within printed p. 98 at the first of two distinct anonymous Middle English verse versions and ends with Kölbing's signature on p. 121; it omits the article introduction on pp. 57–98 but retains Version I from the Auchinleck manuscript, German commentary, Version II from Cotton MS Caligula A II, and a final German typographic note. A complete 560-page Internet Archive PDF of Englische Studien volume 1 and its metadata/scandata sidecars are cached, as are the complete PDF, OCR, metadata, and scandata for volume 7 containing Kölbing's 1884 re-collation corrections to Version I. The NLS Auchinleck project's complete verified 701-image public inventory is cached with target ff. 25r–31v mapped separately. Current official catalogs identify collateral copies in Beinecke MS 365 and Bodleian MS. Eng. poet. d. 208; no complete public image objects were found for Cotton, Beinecke, or the Bodleian guardbook, so mediated whole-object reproduction routes are recorded without treating partial or absent imagery as complete.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is a modern edited composite, not a single homogeneous medieval work.
Kölbing's legacy Cotton foliation and the current British Library locator are both preserved without asserting a crosswalk.
The 1884 corrections are later comparison evidence and are not placed upstream of the U-M encoding.

agents.name: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00066
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00066.
REVDESCR records a 2016-09-13 restructuring that removed prose fragments at the beginning and adjusted proofing and page-break placement.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00066
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text selecting Kölbing's two edited Middle English poems, intervening German commentary, final typographic note, and dateline from printed pp. 98–121 while omitting the article introduction on pp. 57–98.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium (1877)
entities.description: Kölbing's 65-page Englische Studien article, combining a historical and source introduction with editions of two Middle English versions, German notes, and an October 1876 editorial dateline.

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium,' Englische Studien 1 (1877), 57–121.
bibliographic.title: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium

physical_description.extent: printed pp. 57–121 within Englische Studien volume 1
notes: The article begins below the preceding article on p. 57 and ends above the next article on p. 121; the encoded selection begins lower on p. 98 and ends with Kölbing's signature on p. 121.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)
entities.description: The principal and incomplete manuscript witness used for Kölbing's Version I; the work now cataloged as St Patrick's Purgatory and the Knight, Sir Owen occupies ff. 25r–31v, or columns ff. 25ra–31vb in the project transcription.

holding.notes: The current codex contains i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios plus modern paper endleaves; detached fragments survive in other repositories.

physical_description.extent: i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios, with 14 stubs and modern paper endleaves; detached fragments survive elsewhere
notes: Probably produced in London; many leaves and miniatures are lost. Version I lacks its beginning and manuscript title.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: A two-part paper manuscript containing the Version II text Owayne Miles at current ff. 91v–95r in Part 1. Kölbing instead cites legacy f. 89 ff.; the exact foliation shift is not inferred.

physical_description.extent: 210 folios in two parts, with early modern endleaves ff. 1–2 and 140–141
notes: Current binding is a British Museum binding dated 1957. The target work lies between The Fifteen Signs before Doomsday ending on f. 91r and Visio Tundalie beginning on f. 95v.

entities.label: Beinecke Library, MS 365 (Book of Brome)
entities.description: A collateral later-fifteenth-century copy of the Version II tradition: Sir Owen the Knight, IMEV 1767, occupies ff. 28r–38r and is followed by a blank f. 38v.

holding.notes: Restricted material; consultation requires permission of the appropriate curator.

physical_description.support: paper with an original late-fifteenth-century vellum wrapper
physical_description.extent: modern ff. 1–81: 78 leaves plus flyleaves; 12 leaves and one flyleaf missing, with no cataloged loss in the literary texts
notes: The target text was written by Scribe 1 on ff. 28–38. The main texts are associated with East Anglia near the end of the fifteenth century; that witness context is not transferred to composition.

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. d. 208
entities.description: A guardbook of manuscript and printed leaves with commentary by William E. Moss. Part 1 contains collateral Owayne Miles binding fragments mounted as fols. 15, 18, 22, 25, 27, and 28.

date_statements.label: catalog range
date_statements.notes: The broad range covers the guardbook's heterogeneous fragments and is not a date for Owayne Miles alone.

physical_description.support: manuscript and printed leaves mounted in a guardbook
notes: The Middle English leaves were recovered from a binding made by Garret Godfrey of Cambridge (active 1503–1539) and mounted by William E. Moss.

entities.label: David Laing and William Turnbull, Owain Miles and Other Inedited Fragments (1837)
entities.description: The rare prior printed edition used and criticized by Kölbing for Version I; the contemporary edition was limited to 32 copies.

bibliographic.citation: David Laing and William B. D. D. Turnbull, eds., Owain Miles and Other Inedited Fragments of Ancient English Poetry, Collected from MSS. in the Advocates' Library, Edinburgh (Edinburgh: [David Laing], 1837).
bibliographic.title: Owain Miles and Other Inedited Fragments of Ancient English Poetry

notes: The Yale catalog reports an edition of 32 copies.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Vier Romanzen-Handschriften (1884)
entities.description: A later Englische Studien article whose Auchinleck section reports a fresh comparison and corrections to Kölbing's 1877 Version I text on pp. 181–182.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete provider scan of Englische Studien volume 1 (1877)
entities.description: The complete 560-page PDF exposed for Internet Archive item englischestudien01leipuoft. The cached provider object includes the full scanned volume around Kölbing's article; no claim is made that unexposed binding surfaces or preservation masters are included.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete provider scan of Englische Studien volume 7 (1884)
entities.description: The complete 514-page PDF and complete provider OCR exposed for Internet Archive item englische-studien_1884_7, including Kölbing's corrections on printed pp. 181–182; no claim is made for unexposed physical surfaces or preservation masters.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images and OCR text

entities.label: Auchinleck Manuscript project digital facsimile
entities.description: The NLS-supported project exposes a full transcription and facsimile links for 701 surviving codex and detached-fragment surfaces through IIIF Image API services. No Presentation manifest is offered, so the complete verified URL inventory is bundled locally.

physical_description.extent: 701 public codex, stub, and detached-fragment images in the verified project inventory
notes: The inventory contains all provider-published project facsimile links; it does not establish that covers, spine, edges, or preservation masters are exposed.

entities.reproduction_format: Individual IIIF JPEG images and Image Information documents

entities.label: Robert Easting, An edition of Owayne Miles and other Middle English texts concerning St. Patrick's Purgatory (1977)
entities.description: A later Oxford DPhil thesis editing and studying the Middle English St Patrick's Purgatory tradition. It is descriptive and collateral evidence here, not an ancestor of Kölbing's 1877 article or the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Robert Easting, An edition of Owayne Miles and other Middle English texts concerning St. Patrick's Purgatory (DPhil thesis, University of Oxford, 1977).
bibliographic.title: An edition of Owayne Miles and other Middle English texts concerning St. Patrick's Purgatory

entities.label: Robert Easting, St Patrick's Purgatory: Fragments of a Second Copy of the Middle English Stanzaic Owayne Miles (2006)
entities.description: A later scholarly article describing the recovered Bodleian fragments as a second copy of the stanzaic Owayne Miles tradition; it is collateral research evidence only.

bibliographic.citation: Robert Easting, 'St Patrick's Purgatory: Fragments of a Second Copy of the Middle English Stanzaic Owayne Miles,' Medium Ævum 75.1 (2006), 84–102.
bibliographic.title: St Patrick's Purgatory: Fragments of a Second Copy of the Middle English Stanzaic Owayne Miles

editorial_practices.description: The U-M/repository encoding selects printed pp. 98–121 beginning at Version I, omits the article introduction on pp. 57–98 and prose above the poem on p. 98, and retains both poems, the intervening notes, final typographic note, and editorial dateline.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole CME00066 encoding mapped to selected matter in Kölbing's 1877 article
notes: REVDESCR explicitly records removal of prose fragments at the beginning in the 2016 revision.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings in the Auchinleck and Cotton manuscript witnesses and errors in the scarce 1837 prior edition
editorial_practices.representation: Two separately headed edited texts with manuscript readings, supplied loss markers and title, notes, and typographic conventions
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing presents manuscript-based texts of two distinct versions, marks the lost beginning of Version I, compares the versions and prior edition, and explains selected typographic signs rather than silently normalizing them.
editorial_practices.scope: Version I, commentary, Version II, and final typographic note

editorial_practices.source_feature: Fresh comparison of the Auchinleck Owayne Miles against Kölbing's 1877 edition
editorial_practices.representation: A compact correction list on printed pp. 181–182
editorial_practices.description: The 1884 article supplies later corrections to Version I only; those corrections remain a downstream comparison layer until U-M production evidence establishes otherwise.

open_questions.question: How does Kölbing's legacy Cotton locus f. 89 ff. crosswalk to the current British Library locator ff. 91v–95r?
next_steps: Obtain or inspect complete Cotton MS Caligula A II images and a repository foliation concordance; retain current ff. 91v–95r as the authoritative discovery locator meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete, reusable image set for Cotton MS Caligula A II?
next_steps: Request all provider-exposed physical components through the digitisation service—binding boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, and all 210 folios in both parts—while mapping target ff. 91v–95r separately and asking for image count, cost, restrictions, and reuse terms.

open_questions.question: Will the Beinecke curator authorize consultation or complete-object photography of restricted MS 365, and on what reuse terms?
next_steps: Request the entire physical object—original vellum wrapper, sewing, exposed turn-ins/pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and all leaves—with target ff. 28r–38r mapped separately; obtain image-count, completeness, cost, and publication terms before reuse.

open_questions.question: Can Bodleian Libraries supply a complete physical-guardbook image set for MS. Eng. poet. d. 208 and clarify the Owayne Miles fragment sequence and reuse terms?
next_steps: Request binding/boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, guards, and all mounted leaves, with target fols. 15, 18, 22, 25, 27, and 28 mapped separately; ask for exact sequence, overlap with the Auchinleck text, image count, cost, and reuse terms.

open_questions.question: What exact intermediary and stemmatic relationships connect the two Middle English versions to each other and to the Latin Tractatus tradition?
next_steps: Review modern source studies and critical editions beyond landing-page abstracts; preserve Kölbing's cautious fuller-common-source hypothesis and do not assert direct derivation between the two versions without stronger evidence.

open_questions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains selection of printed pp. 98–121 and the 2016 removal of opening prose fragments?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for selection, keying, proofreading, and revision notes while retaining the directly observed source mapping.
